transaction.hash: A unique identifier for this transaction, generated from its contents.
transaction.from: The address that initiated and signed this transaction.
transaction.status: Whether the transaction executed successfully or reverted.
transaction.confirmations: Blocks mined after this transaction. More confirmations means higher finality.
transaction.interactedWith: The contract this transaction called, or the recipient address for simple transfers.
transaction.transactionFee: Total fee paid to process this transaction (gas used × gas price). Gas measures computational work on the network, and under EIP-1559 fees include a base fee (burned) plus a priority fee (tip).
transaction.gasPrice: Price per unit of gas, in Gwei. Higher gas prices incentivize faster inclusion.
transaction.gasLimitUsage: Gas limit is the maximum gas authorized. Gas used is the actual amount consumed.
transaction.nonce: Sender's transaction counter. Ensures transactions are processed in order and prevents replay attacks.
transaction.position: The index of this transaction within its block. Lower positions were processed first.
transaction.type: The transaction format (e.g. Legacy, EIP-1559, EIP-4844). Determines which fee fields apply.
transaction.decodedInput: The function call and parameters sent to the contract, decoded into human-readable form.
transaction.maxFeePerBlobGas: Maximum price per unit of blob gas the sender is willing to pay for data availability.
transaction.blobGasPrice: Actual price per unit of blob gas paid in this block.
transaction.blobGasUsed: Amount of blob gas consumed by this transaction's data blobs. Blobs are data containers introduced in EIP-4844 for rollup data availability, priced separately from execution gas.
transaction.blobCount: Number of data containers attached to this transaction for rollup data availability.
transaction.blobVersionedHashes: Versioned hashes that uniquely identify each data blob committed by this transaction.
transaction.contractCreated: The address of the new contract deployed by this transaction.
transaction.value: Amount of native currency (e.g. ETH) transferred in this transaction.
transaction.effectiveGasPrice: The actual gas price paid after EIP-1559 fee calculation, which may differ from the requested price.
transaction.l1BlockNumber: The Ethereum L1 block that anchors this L2 transaction for security.
transaction.gasUsedForL1: Gas consumed for posting this transaction's data to Ethereum L1.
transaction.l1Fee: Fee paid for data availability on Ethereum L1. L2 networks rely on L1 to publish transaction data for security and verification.
transaction.l1GasPrice: The L1 gas price used to calculate the data availability fee component.
transaction.l1GasUsed: Estimated L1 gas required to post this transaction's calldata.
transaction.l1FeeScalar: A multiplier applied to the L1 fee calculation by the L2 sequencer.
transaction.txHashColumn: The unique hash identifying this transaction.
transaction.blockColumn: The block number this transaction was included in.
transaction.methodColumn: The smart contract function called by this transaction.
transaction.fromColumn: The address that sent this transaction.
transaction.toColumn: The destination address of this transaction.
transaction.valueColumn: The amount of native currency transferred.
transaction.statusColumn: Whether the transaction succeeded or failed.
transaction.callTree: Trace of all internal calls made during this transaction's execution.
transaction.gasProfiler: Breakdown of gas consumption across each internal call.
transaction.stateChanges: Blockchain state modifications (balances, nonces, storage) caused by this transaction.
transaction.balanceChange: Change in native currency balance for this address caused by the transaction.
transaction.nonceChange: Change in transaction count (nonce) for this address.
transaction.codeDeployed: New contract bytecode deployed at this address by the transaction.
transaction.storageChange: A storage slot in the contract's state that was modified by the transaction.

block.hash: A unique fingerprint identifying this block, derived from its contents.
block.transactions: The number of transactions included and executed in this block.
block.withdrawals: Validator withdrawals processed in this block, returning staked ETH.
block.feeRecipient: The address that receives priority fees from transactions in this block.
block.difficulty: Mining difficulty for this block. Zero on proof-of-stake networks after the Merge. On some L2 networks, this may be a non-zero constant.
block.totalDifficulty: Cumulative difficulty of the chain up to this block. Legacy field from proof-of-work.
block.size: The total size of the block data in bytes.
block.extraData: Arbitrary data set by the block producer. Often contains client or pool identifiers.
block.gasUsed: Total gas consumed by all transactions in this block.
block.gasLimit: Maximum gas allowed in this block, defining its capacity.
block.baseFeePerGas: Minimum gas price for this block, set by the network based on demand (EIP-1559). This base fee is burned.
block.burntFees: Transaction fees permanently removed from circulation via EIP-1559.
block.blobGasUsed: Total blob gas consumed by blob transactions in this block.
block.excessBlobGas: Blob gas above the target, used to calculate the next block's blob base fee.
block.blobCount: Number of data containers included in this block for rollup data availability.
block.l1BlockNumber: The Ethereum L1 block number associated with this Arbitrum block.
block.sendCount: Number of outgoing L2-to-L1 messages sent in this Arbitrum block.
block.sendRoot: Merkle root of all outgoing L2-to-L1 messages, used for cross-layer verification.
block.parentHash: Hash of the previous block in the chain, linking blocks together.
block.stateRoot: Merkle root of the entire blockchain state (a cryptographic summary of all account data) after processing this block.
block.transactionsRoot: Merkle root of all transactions included in this block.
block.receiptsRoot: Merkle root of all transaction receipts from this block.
block.withdrawalsRoot: Merkle root of all validator withdrawals processed in this block.
block.logsBloom: A bloom filter (probabilistic data structure) for efficiently searching event logs emitted in this block.
block.nonce: A value used in proof-of-work mining. Always zero after the Merge.
block.mixHash: A hash used in the proof-of-work algorithm. Replaced by RANDAO value after the Merge.
block.sha3Uncles: Hash of the uncle blocks list. Always the empty list hash after the Merge.
block.validator: The validator index that initiated this withdrawal from the beacon chain.
block.finalized: This block has been finalized by the network's consensus mechanism and cannot be reverted.

address.verification: A verified contract has its source code publicly confirmed to match the deployed bytecode.
address.proxyType: This contract uses an upgradeable proxy pattern, where execution is delegated to a separate implementation contract, allowing upgrades without changing the address.
address.readContract: Query contract data without spending gas or connecting a wallet. Read-only calls.
address.writeContract: Send a transaction that changes contract state. Requires a connected wallet and gas.
address.balance: Native currency balance held by this address.
address.usdValue: Estimated fiat value based on current market price.
address.nonce: Number of transactions sent from this address. Used for transaction ordering.
address.eip7702Delegate: This account delegates its code execution to another address via EIP-7702.
address.implementationAddress: The contract that contains the actual logic for this proxy.
address.ensName: Ethereum Name Service (maps human-readable names to addresses) name associated with this address.
address.ensApp: Application or resolver linked to this ENS name.
address.accountType: The type of this address. EOA (Externally Owned Account) is a regular wallet controlled by a private key. Contract is code deployed on-chain.
address.contractBytecode: The compiled EVM bytecode deployed at this address.
address.sourceCode: The original Solidity/Vyper source code, verified to match the deployed bytecode.
address.rawAbi: The Application Binary Interface (ABI) defines how to interact with this contract's functions.
address.functions: Available contract functions that can be called to read data or modify state.

token.tokenStandard: The token interface standard (e.g. ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-1155) that defines how this token behaves.
token.decimals: Number of decimal places used to display token amounts. Most ERC-20 tokens use 18.
token.totalSupply: Total number of tokens that have been created for this contract.
token.tokenId: A unique numeric identifier for this specific token within its collection.
token.owner: The address that currently holds this NFT.
token.approved: An address authorized to transfer this specific NFT on behalf of the owner.
token.metadataUri: The URI where this token's metadata (name, image, attributes) is stored.

bitcoin.txid: A unique identifier for this transaction, computed from its serialized data.
bitcoin.witnessHash: Transaction hash including witness (SegWit) data. Differs from TXID for SegWit transactions.
bitcoin.block: The block that includes this transaction.
bitcoin.status: Whether this transaction has been confirmed in a block or is still waiting in the mempool.
bitcoin.inputs: The number of previous outputs being spent. Each input references a UTXO. Bitcoin uses a UTXO model where transactions consume and create discrete outputs.
bitcoin.outputs: The number of new outputs created. Each output locks BTC to an address.
bitcoin.fee: The difference between total inputs and total outputs, paid to the miner.
bitcoin.feePerByte: Fee rate in satoshis per byte of raw transaction data.
bitcoin.feePerVByte: Fee rate in satoshis per virtual byte. The standard fee metric for SegWit transactions.
bitcoin.feePerWU: Fee rate in satoshis per weight unit. The most granular fee metric.
bitcoin.size: Raw transaction size in bytes, including witness data.
bitcoin.virtualSize: Adjusted transaction size that discounts witness data. Used for fee calculation.
bitcoin.weight: Transaction weight in weight units (WU). 1 vByte = 4 WU.
bitcoin.coinbase: Whether this is a coinbase transaction that creates new BTC as a block reward.
bitcoin.witness: Whether this transaction uses Segregated Witness (SegWit) format.
bitcoin.rbf: Replace-By-Fee. If enabled, this transaction can be replaced by one with a higher fee before confirmation.
bitcoin.version: Transaction format version number. Determines which features are available.
bitcoin.locktime: Earliest block height or time when this transaction can be included in a block.
bitcoin.blockHash: The unique hash identifying the block that contains this transaction.
bitcoin.minedBy: The mining pool or entity that produced this block.
bitcoin.blockReward: New BTC created as a reward for mining this block (subsidy + fees).
bitcoin.totalFees: Sum of all transaction fees collected by the miner in this block.
bitcoin.feeRate: Average and median fee rates of transactions in this block.
bitcoin.transactions: Number of transactions included in this block.
bitcoin.totalOutput: Total BTC value of all outputs created in this block.
bitcoin.difficulty: How hard it is to find a valid block hash. Adjusts every 2016 blocks.
bitcoin.blockSize: Total size of the serialized block data.
bitcoin.blockWeight: Block weight in weight units. Maximum is 4,000,000 WU.
bitcoin.previousBlock: Hash of the preceding block, linking this block to the chain.
bitcoin.nextBlock: Hash of the following block, if one exists.
bitcoin.coinbaseMessage: Arbitrary text embedded in the coinbase transaction by the miner.
bitcoin.merkleRoot: Root hash of the Merkle tree (a structure that allows efficient verification) of all transactions in this block.
bitcoin.blockVersion: Block format version. Encodes which consensus rules and soft forks are signaled.
bitcoin.bits: Compact encoding of the target threshold for a valid block hash.
bitcoin.blockNonce: A value miners iterate to find a block hash below the target.
bitcoin.coinbaseHex: Raw hexadecimal data of the coinbase transaction input.
bitcoin.address: A Bitcoin address derived from a public key, used to receive BTC.
bitcoin.balance: Total BTC held by this address across all unspent outputs.
bitcoin.totalReceived: Cumulative BTC received by this address across all transactions.
bitcoin.utxos: Unspent Transaction Outputs. Individual coins available to be spent by this address.
bitcoin.txCount: Total number of transactions involving this address.
bitcoin.blockConfirmations: Blocks mined after this block. More confirmations means the block is more deeply embedded in the chain.
bitcoin.confirmations: Blocks mined after the block containing this transaction. More confirmations means higher finality.
bitcoin.inputsColumn: Previous transaction outputs being spent. Each input references a UTXO (Unspent Transaction Output).
bitcoin.outputsColumn: New outputs created by this transaction. Each output locks BTC to a recipient address.

network.gasPrice: Current cost per unit of gas on this network.
network.blockNumber: The most recently mined block number on this network.
network.syncStatus: Whether the connected node has fully synced with the network.
network.clientVersion: The software client and version running on the connected node.
network.protocolVersion: The Ethereum protocol version supported by the connected node.
network.currencyPrice: Current market price of this network's native currency.
network.latestBlock: The most recently produced block on this network.

settings.knowledgeLevel: Controls how much explanatory help is shown throughout the explorer.
